description: SLB is a global energy technology and oilfield services company supplying software, equipment, subsea systems, and operational services for hydrocarbon development and energy transition projects.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: SLB, formerly Schlumberger, sits near the center of the upstream energy stack: exploration, drilling, production, reservoir management, digital operations, and subsea infrastructure. Its customers are mostly oil and gas producers, with SLB selling the specialized tools, software, field services, and engineering capacity that make difficult reservoirs less speculative and more executable.

The company’s positioning is no longer just rigs, sensors, and pressure-control hardware. SLB increasingly describes itself as an energy technology company, which is a polished way of saying it still serves fossil-fuel production while adding digital platforms, carbon management, and lower-emission operations to the pitch.

overview.title: Digital And Software
paragraphs: Delfi is SLB’s cloud-based digital platform for exploration and production workflows. It brings together subsurface interpretation, drilling, production, data management, AI-assisted workflows, and domain applications in a SaaS environment aimed at reducing cycle times and improving technical decision-making.

For the registry, Delfi is the most obvious software product family to map because it combines proprietary domain models, customer operational data, cloud infrastructure, and AI tooling. Any free or open alternative analysis should separate general-purpose open data and modeling tools from the highly specialized, compliance-heavy workflows used by large energy operators.

overview.title: Subsea Systems
paragraphs: OneSubsea is SLB’s subsea technology and solutions business, structured as a joint venture with Aker Solutions and Subsea7. It focuses on subsea production systems, processing, tiebacks, field delivery, and related engineering for offshore developments.

This business is more hardware-and-project intensive than Delfi, but it still matters for Free The World because the dependency surface includes proprietary control systems, engineering standards, vendor-locked equipment ecosystems, and operational know-how that is not easily replaced by a GitHub repo and optimism.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: SLB should be treated as a hybrid of industrial services, proprietary software, energy infrastructure, and applied AI. The open-alternative question is therefore not simply whether there is a free clone of Delfi or OneSubsea, but where open standards, open data formats, open simulation tools, transparent emissions accounting, and interoperable industrial systems can reduce lock-in.

Initial research should prioritize SLB’s digital platform architecture, its OneSubsea ownership and product scope, and current investor disclosures that show which segments are strategically important rather than merely legacy revenue machinery.
